title: Maruta cotula
taxonomy: Maruta cotula is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It belongs to the genus Maruta, which is a group of flowering plants. The species Maruta cotula is often classified as a weed or a wild relative of cultivated Brassica species. The classification of Maruta cotula is further supported by its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. By examining its classification, researchers can better understand the evolutionary relationships between different species within the Brassicaceae family.

properties: Maruta cotula is commonly known as Spotted Mayweed or Spotted Chamomile. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its potential therapeutic properties. Maruta cotula is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which may help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation. There is no information available on a plant called Maruta cotula. However, Maruta is a genus of plants in the family Rutaceae, commonly known as citrus or rue family. 

If you meant to ask about Maruta (also known as rue or Ruta graveolens), here's a paragraph about it:

Ruta graveolens, commonly known as rue, is a perennial herb native to the Old World. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, which can help in treating various health issues such as arthritis, wounds, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of rue include sesquiterpenoids, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Rue is commonly prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or cream for topical application, and it's also used in aromatherapy to relieve stress and improve mood.
